metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either name a specific registered scorer from `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` that is applicable to `ticket_digest` output, or explicitly prescribe adding a new `emits_valid_ticket_digest` scorer to `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` (and its implementation) as part of this story's scope, so the eval spec can pass `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)`.
findings.finding: The eval spec is required to pass `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)`, which rejects any deterministic scorer name not registered in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` (`known_scorer_names()`). The registry contains scorers only for `review_result`, `completion_verdict`, and `verification` output shapes — none of which match a `ticket_digest` payload. The plan does not specify which registered scorer name(s) to use in the `enrich.eval.yaml`, nor does it prescribe adding a new scorer to the registry. Any scorer name the author invents will be silently accepted by lenient validation but hard-rejected by `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)`, causing the eval CI gate to fail.
findings.location: Testing section: 'Eval `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/enrich.eval.yaml`… the spec must register ≥1 deterministic scorer per `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `eval_scorers.py::REGISTRY` must be extended with at least one new scorer for the `ticket_digest` output shape (e.g. `digest_schema_valid`) before the eval spec can pass `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)`.
findings.finding: The eval spec's deterministic scorers will fail `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` because the `ticket_digest` output shape is not handled by any registered scorer in `eval_scorers.py::REGISTRY`, and strict validation rejects scorer names absent from `known_scorer_names()`.
findings.location: Testing section / Eval spec requirement: 'the spec must register ≥1 deterministic scorer per validate_eval_spec(strict=True)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a scope note under Testing: 'Add a `digest_schema_valid` scorer to `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py::REGISTRY` that validates the op output against the `ticket_digest` schema; reference it by that name in `enrich.eval.yaml`. This is required for `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` to accept the spec.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the deterministic scorer(s) the `enrich.eval.yaml` will declare and confirm they either already exist in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` or specify that a new scorer (e.g. `emits_valid_ticket_digest`) must be added to `eval_scorers.py` as part of this story's scope.
findings.finding: The eval spec requires '≥1 registered deterministic scorer' that passes via `rebar prompt eval enrich`, but the existing `eval_scorers.py` REGISTRY contains no scorer for `ticket_digest` schema validation. All existing deterministic scorers are typed to `review_result`, `completion_verdict`, or `verification` shapes. The plan does not name the scorer(s) the spec will declare or acknowledge that a new scorer must be added to `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` before `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` will pass — this is an unverified capability assertion.
findings.location: ## Testing — 'Eval … the spec must register ≥1 deterministic scorer per validate_eval_spec(strict=True)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'Add scorer `emits_valid_ticket_digest` to `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py` — this scorer validates that the op output conforms to `ticket_digest.schema.json` (all four required fields present, arrays of non-empty strings, propositions minItems 1).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py` to the scope as an edit target, specifying a new deterministic scorer (e.g. `emits_valid_ticket_digest`) that validates ticket_digest output shape, and register it in `REGISTRY` so `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` accepts the eval spec.
findings.finding: The eval spec requires '≥1 registered deterministic scorer' that satisfies `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)`, but no scorer in the existing `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` can validate `ticket_digest` output — all registered scorers target `review_result` (findings list), `completion_verdict` (PASS/FAIL verdict), code-review findings, or verifier outputs. The plan never specifies that a new scorer must be registered.
findings.location: Testing section: 'Eval … the spec must register ≥1 deterministic scorer per validate_eval_spec(strict=True)'; AC: 'Eval spec (packaged, ≥6 independently-labeled tickets, ≥1 registered deterministic scorer) passes via rebar prompt eval enrich'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'New scorer `emits_valid_ticket_digest` in `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py`: validates that output is a dict with all four required fields and arrays of non-empty strings; register in REGISTRY'. Name this scorer in the eval spec's scorers list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `ticket_digest`-specific deterministic scorer (e.g. `emits_valid_ticket_digest`) to `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py`'s REGISTRY that validates the four required fields and array constraints, and reference it by name in `enrich.eval.yaml`.
findings.finding: The plan mandates `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` passes for the enrich eval, but strict mode rejects any deterministic scorer name not registered in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY`. No existing scorer in the registry (`emits_valid_review_result`, `emits_valid_verdict`, `emits_valid_verification`, `code_review_emits_valid_findings`, etc.) applies to a `ticket_digest`-shaped output `{problem_keywords, component_or_area, key_entities, propositions}`. The plan does not specify registering a new scorer in `eval_scorers.py`, so the eval spec will fail strict validation out of the box.
findings.location: Testing section / AC bullet 6: 'eval spec … ≥1 registered deterministic scorer … validate_eval_spec(strict=True)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the valid `category` value for `enrich.md`: either name the specific existing category that is appropriate, or explicitly permit adding a new category value (e.g. `enrichment`) and describe any index/catalog changes that requires.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the implementer to pick the prompt 'category' from the 'prompt library's closed category set (do NOT invent one; pick an existing member)', but none of the existing categories — 'review', 'plan-review-criterion', 'plan-review-pass', 'code-review-pass' — is semantically appropriate for a ticket-enrichment feature extractor. 'review' would wrongly list 'enrich' as a reviewer in the catalog (load_catalog / select_reviewers iterate only category='review' prompts); 'plan-review-criterion'/'plan-review-pass'/'code-review-pass' are equally wrong for an enrichment op. The implementer will either violate the 'do NOT invent' constraint or misclassify the prompt, both of which cause downstream failures (the enrich prompt would appear in reviewer selection, or the prompt library's category-validation logic would warn/fail).
findings.location: Scope section — 'Prompt src/rebar/llm/reviewers/enrich.md, front-matter … a category value from the prompt library's closed category set (do NOT invent one; pick an existing member)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `category: enrichment` as a new category (alongside a note that it does not populate the reviewer catalog — only `review` does — and does not require a `regenerate_prompt_index` step since non-reviewer prompts are excluded from `build_prompt_index`). Alternatively, allow `category:` to be absent/null for non-reviewer, non-criterion prompts, which `get_prompt` already handles gracefully (category is stored as None).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the `completion.py` model reference with the correct reference module that actually demonstrates `RunRequest(mode="structured", execution_mode="single_turn")` construction (e.g. a `RunnerAgentStep` in the workflow engine, or a clear standalone example), since `completion.py` delegates to `gate_dispatch` and never builds a `RunRequest`.
findings.finding: The plan claims `enrich.py` should be 'Modeled on `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` (assemble context → load prompt → `RunRequest(mode="structured", execution_mode="single_turn")`)' but `completion.py` does NOT use `RunRequest` directly at all — it delegates entirely to `gate_dispatch.produce_completion_verdict` via the workflow engine, and contains no `RunRequest` construction. The `RunRequest(mode="structured", execution_mode="single_turn")` pattern lives in the workflow `RunnerAgentStep`, not in `completion.py`. An implementer following this false model reference may look in the wrong place for the actual structured/single-turn dispatch pattern.
findings.location: What section: 'Modeled on `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` (assemble context → load prompt → `RunRequest(mode="structured", execution_mode="single_turn")`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Point to the workflow RunnerAgentStep (src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py or similar) where RunRequest with mode='structured' and execution_mode='single_turn' is actually assembled, or simply inline the pattern: 'build RunRequest(system_prompt=..., instructions=..., config=cfg, mode="structured", execution_mode="single_turn", output_schema="ticket_digest")' directly in the spec.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact return type/shape of `enrich()` — e.g. a named dataclass `EnrichResult(digest: dict, low_proposition_count: bool)` or a wrapper dict — so `test_propositions_bounded` and downstream consumers have a concrete contract.
findings.finding: The `low_proposition_count` flag's location on the return value of `enrich()` is unspecified: the `ticket_digest` schema has exactly four fields (none of them the flag), but the plan does not describe a wrapper type or side-channel, leaving the executor to invent the return shape.
findings.location: Scope — 'Config-bound threading' section: 'set a low_proposition_count flag on the result (never raise)'; also AC: 'propositions count enforced in the op (>max truncate, <min flag, no raise)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope section: 'enrich() returns EnrichResult(digest: TicketDigest, low_proposition_count: bool); the ticket_digest schema covers only the digest payload; the flag is on the wrapper and never serialized to the schema.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify which specific category value the `enrich.md` prompt should declare, or explicitly add a new category (e.g. `enrichment`) to the closed set and document it as the correct choice for structured-extraction prompts — since no existing category in the closed set (`review`, `code-review-pass`, `plan-review-criterion`, `plan-review-pass`) is semantically correct for a non-reviewer structured extractor.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the prompt author to 'pick an existing member' of the prompt library's closed `category` set but does not name which one. Choosing `category: review` would cause `build_prompt_index` / `load_catalog` to include the `enrich` prompt in the reviewer selection catalog (only `category == "review"` entries populate it), making it appear as a selectable reviewer for `review_ticket` — which is incorrect for a structured extractor. The other categories (`code-review-pass`, `plan-review-criterion`, `plan-review-pass`) are workflow-pass categories that are also semantically wrong. None of the existing closed-set members fit a standalone structured-extraction prompt.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Prompt `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/enrich.md`, … a `category` value from the prompt library's closed category set (do NOT invent one; pick an existing member)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `category` ambiguity for `enrich.md`: either identify which existing category value is semantically correct for a feature-extractor (if one exists), or explicitly permit introducing a new category value (e.g. `enrich`) and update the closed category set accordingly.
findings.finding: The `category` field for `enrich.md` is underspecified: the plan says 'pick an existing member of the closed category set' but no existing category fits a feature-extractor prompt — using `category: review` would incorrectly add the prompt to the reviewer catalog (`load_catalog` / `is_reviewer = True`), and the other categories (`code-review-pass`, `plan-review-criterion`, `plan-review-pass`) are equally wrong semantically.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Prompt src/rebar/llm/reviewers/enrich.md, front-matter ... a category value from the prompt library's closed category set (do NOT invent one; pick an existing member)'
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the 'do NOT invent one' constraint and introduce a new category value `enrich` (or `feature-extractor`) to the closed set, adding it to FRONT_MATTER_KEYS documentation. Alternatively, if the prompt should not be registered in the reviewer catalog at all, clarify that `category` may be omitted or set to a sentinel that leaves is_reviewer=False.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific `category` value the `enrich.md` prompt should declare (or explicitly authorize adding a new category value like `enrichment` to the closed set), so the executor does not have to infer it from codebase archaeology.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the executor to pick a `category` value for the prompt front-matter from the 'prompt library's closed category set' but never names the valid members of that set. The observed closed set in the codebase is: `review`, `code-review-pass`, `plan-review-criterion`, `plan-review-pass`. None of these obviously maps to an enrichment op that produces a structured digest (not a verdict or review findings), leaving an unresolved capability/compatibility question — using the wrong category value may exclude the prompt from unexpected index lookups or cause validation failures.
findings.location: ## Scope — 'a `category` value from the prompt library's closed category set (do NOT invent one; pick an existing member)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line such as: 'Use `category: enrichment` (a new category value — add it to the closed set in `prompts_frontmatter.py` and document its semantics) since no existing category maps to a structured-extraction op that produces a digest rather than a review verdict.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the `low_proposition_count` flag with the `ticket_digest` schema: either add the field to the schema (with `type: boolean`, `default: false`) or specify a wrapper envelope (e.g. `{digest: ticket_digest, meta: {low_proposition_count: bool}}`) and update the AC/tests accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan describes `low_proposition_count` as a flag 'set on the result' returned by the op, but the `ticket_digest` schema (the only output schema defined) does not include this field, and the plan does not state whether `additionalProperties` is allowed or the flag lives in a separate wrapper. An implementer following the schema spec would produce a payload that either fails validation (if the flag is added) or silently drops it.
findings.location: Config-bound threading section ('set a `low_proposition_count` flag on the result') vs. Output schema section ('ticket_digest: {problem_keywords, component_or_area, key_entities, propositions}') vs. Scope ('register in contracts.py via register_contract')
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"low_proposition_count": {"type": "boolean", "default": false}` to `ticket_digest.schema.json` (mark it `required` or give it a default), and note in the schema spec that this field is populated by the op post-validation step, not by the LLM output.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact valid `category` value to use in `enrich.md` front-matter (or cite the file/constant that defines the closed set and state the chosen member) so the executor has no ambiguity.
findings.finding: The prompt front-matter `category` field must come from a closed set but the plan neither names the set nor links to where it lives, forcing the executor to discover the valid values independently; picking a wrong value would cause registration failure.
findings.location: Scope — Prompt `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/enrich.md` bullet: 'a `category` value from the prompt library's closed category set (do NOT invent one; pick an existing member)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line such as: 'use `category: extraction` (or whichever existing member is appropriate; valid values live in `src/rebar/llm/prompts.py::CategoryEnum`)' — pick the member and commit to it in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify exactly where `low_proposition_count` lives in the op's return value: either declare it as an optional boolean field in `ticket_digest.schema.json` (and in the Pydantic contract builder), or define a wrapper envelope (e.g. `{digest: <ticket_digest>, low_proposition_count: bool}`) and update AC-1 and test_schema_valid to match.
findings.finding: The `low_proposition_count` flag's structural home in the result is unspecified relative to `validate_structured()`'s fail-closed JSON Schema check: if the flag is placed inside the schema-validated dict, it will be rejected (the four-field ticket_digest schema does not declare it); if it is placed outside the validated payload, the plan needs to specify that wrapper structure.
findings.location: Config-bound threading section: 'if FEWER than min, keep them and set a low_proposition_count flag on the result (never raise)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify where `low_proposition_count` lives: either add it as an optional field to the `ticket_digest` schema definition (e.g. `low_proposition_count: boolean, optional`), or define it as a field on a wrapper envelope that the op returns alongside the validated digest.
findings.finding: The `low_proposition_count` flag is referenced as being 'set on the result' when <min propositions are returned, but the ticket_digest schema as defined has exactly four fields (`problem_keywords`, `component_or_area`, `key_entities`, `propositions`) with no `low_proposition_count` field — leaving the flag's placement in the schema (or a wrapper envelope) completely unspecified.
findings.location: Config-bound threading section: 'if FEWER than min, keep them and set a `low_proposition_count` flag on the result (never raise)'; Scope section: 'Output schema src/rebar/schemas/ticket_digest.schema.json (field-level: all four fields required)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `low_proposition_count: { type: 'boolean', description: 'True when fewer than overlap_propositions_min propositions were returned' }` as an optional field to `ticket_digest.schema.json`, update the four-field 'all required' language to 'four content fields required; low_proposition_count is optional', and update `test_propositions_bounded` to assert its presence in the returned dict.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (and corresponding unit test, e.g. `test_config_env_override`) verifying that `LLMConfig.from_env` correctly reads `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN`/`REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MAX` and that the defaults are 2/6.
findings.finding: The `LLMConfig` extension (adding `overlap_propositions_min`/`overlap_propositions_max` with env-var binding) is a described deliverable in the Scope section but has no covering acceptance criterion. No AC or test verifies that the fields are read from `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN/MAX` or that their defaults (2/6) are correct.
findings.location: Scope section ('`LLMConfig` … add `overlap_propositions_min` / `overlap_propositions_max` to the `from_env` key set') vs. Acceptance Criteria (no LLMConfig AC)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a reference (ticket ID, commit SHA, or linked document) to the experiment that produced the recall@20=1.0 result so reviewers can verify fixture scope, corpus size, and BM25F configuration match what this story ships.
findings.finding: The plan's primary capability justification — 'recall spike showed digest-BM25F retrieves 8/8 heavy paraphrases at rank 1 (recall@20=1.0)' — is cited only as 'see epic experiment comment' with no ticket ID, URL, or reproducibility path. This is the stated reason the digest is 'the load-bearing artifact of the whole feature', yet reviewers cannot verify whether the experiment covered a representative fixture set, what the ticket corpus was, or whether the BM25F indexing configuration is the one this story ships.
findings.location: ## Why — 'DERISKED: the recall spike (see epic experiment comment) showed digest-BM25F retrieves 8/8 heavy paraphrases at rank 1 (recall@20 = 1.0)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'see epic experiment comment' with the concrete ticket ID or document link, e.g. 'see experiment log on epic <id>, commit <sha>, N=<n> tickets, corpus=rebar+snap'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command (e.g. a unit test `test_config_env_vars`) that sets `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN/MAX` in the environment and asserts `LLMConfig.from_env()` returns the overridden values, wiring through to the op's truncation/flag behavior.
findings.finding: No proving command verifies that the `LLMConfig.from_env` additions (`REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN/MAX`) are actually read from environment variables and wired into the op; `test_propositions_bounded` likely uses hardcoded config objects and would pass even if the env-var read path is broken.
findings.location: Scope — LLMConfig section; Testing — `test_propositions_bounded`; AC: 'propositions count enforced in the op (>max truncate, <min flag, no raise)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Testing: 'test_config_env_vars: monkeypatch REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MIN=1 and REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_PROPOSITIONS_MAX=3, call LLMConfig.from_env(), assert .overlap_propositions_min==1 and .overlap_propositions_max==3.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief ADR or CLAUDE.md section capturing the Cupid op pattern (structured/single_turn vs. agentic/findings), the config-bound post-validation approach for schema builders, and the rationale for LLM-as-feature-extractor over embeddings.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel Cupid-pattern enrichment op (new op type, new config category, new schema-registration pattern, new post-validation approach to config-bound schema) with no mention of capturing this architectural decision in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc.
findings.location: ## What / ## Config-bound threading / ## Scope
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `docs/adr/XXX-cupid-enrich-op-pattern.md` (or a CLAUDE.md section under `rebar.llm`) covering: (1) why enrich uses mode='structured'/execution_mode='single_turn'; (2) the config-bound post-validation pattern for proposition bounds; (3) the Cupid dedup rationale with a link to the recall spike result.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state whether retry/backoff/timeout is inherited from completion.py or must be added to enrich.py, and add a test or AC line covering at least one transient-failure scenario (e.g., timeout or rate-limit).
findings.finding: The plan specifies clean error handling for absent-LLM and malformed JSON, but does not address retry/backoff for transient LLM failures (network errors, rate limits, timeouts), nor does it confirm whether completion.py's retry behavior is inherited by the new op.
findings.location: ## What / ## Acceptance Criteria (test_bad_json / test_absent_llm AC line)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence under '## What': 'Error handling follows completion.py's retry/backoff contract (inherited via RunRequest); enrich.py adds no additional retry logic.' OR if completion.py has no retry, add a `test_transient_llm_error` unit test with a FakeRunner that raises a retryable error and confirm graceful surfacing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability requirement: log (at WARN level) when `low_proposition_count` is set, and log/metric when the LLM call fails, so operators can detect enrichment degradation without manual inspection.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a `low_proposition_count` flag and LLM error states but mentions no observability (logging, metrics, or tracing) for these new failure/degradation points.
findings.location: ## Config-bound threading / ## Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: In enrich.py, emit `logger.warning('low_proposition_count', ticket_id=..., count=...)` when the flag is set, and a structured log entry on LLM error paths. Optionally add a counter metric (e.g., `rebar.enrich.low_proposition_count.total`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the idempotency guarantee for `register_contract`: document (or implement) that registering the same contract name twice is either a no-op (idempotent) or raises a clear `DuplicateContractError`, and confirm the underlying registry structure is safe for concurrent module import.
findings.finding: `register_contract('ticket_digest', <builder>)` is a global registry mutation, but the plan never specifies what happens on duplicate registration (e.g., if `enrich.py` is imported multiple times in the same process or during parallel test collection): does it raise, silently overwrite, or deduplicate?
findings.location: Scope section — `src/rebar/llm/contracts.py` via `register_contract('ticket_digest', <builder>)`
findings.suggested_fix: In `contracts.py`, make `register_contract` idempotent: if the name already maps to the same builder, no-op; if it maps to a different builder, raise `DuplicateContractError`. Document this in the function's docstring.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `enrich` itself persists the digest or only returns it: if it persists, define the storage target, ownership, and idempotency policy (e.g., upsert on `ticket_id`); if it only returns the payload (leaving persistence to callers), remove or rephrase the 'stored record' language to avoid ambiguity.
findings.finding: The plan uses the phrase "the stored record is stable modulo LLM wording" implying the op's output is persisted somewhere, but never defines where that record is stored, who owns the write, or what happens on re-run (overwrite vs. append — idempotency under retry).
findings.location: What section — "Deterministic payload (no timestamps/uuids) so the stored record is stable modulo LLM wording."

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `category` value for `enrich.md` from the closed set AND confirm whether `reviewers/index.json` must be regenerated and committed (required if `category: review`; verify against `test_index_on_disk_matches_regenerated`).
findings.finding: Placing `enrich.md` in `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/` will be scanned by `build_prompt_index()`, which triggers the existing drift-gate test `tests/unit/test_prompt_index.py::test_index_on_disk_matches_regenerated`. If `category: review`, the prompt enters the index and the committed `reviewers/index.json` must be regenerated — but the plan does not mention this. This is an unenumerated consumer outside the artifact dir.
findings.location: Scope section: `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/enrich.md`
findings.suggested_fix: Use a non-`review` category (e.g. `category: enrichment` or `category: plan-review-pass` if it fits) so `enrich.md` is invisible to `build_prompt_index` and the index stays stable — OR add `regenerate_prompt_index` as an explicit edit target in Scope. Also confirm the prompt need not appear in the reviewer catalog since `enrich` is a structured-extraction op, not a review op.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `enrich.md`'s `category` value is NOT `review` (to stay out of `load_catalog`), or explicitly acknowledge and update `test_every_reviewer_has_execution_mode_stamped` to allow `single_turn` reviewers.
findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_prompt_index.py::test_every_reviewer_has_execution_mode_stamped` iterates every entry in `load_catalog()` and asserts `execution_mode == "agentic"`. If `enrich.md` is placed in `reviewers/` with `category: review` AND `execution_mode: single_turn`, this existing test will fail. This unenumerated consumer is outside the artifact dir.
findings.location: Scope section: `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/enrich.md`, front-matter `execution_mode: single_turn`
findings.suggested_fix: Since `enrich` is a structured-extraction op (not a ticket/code reviewer), use a non-`review` category. This resolves both the index drift and the agentic-mode assertion simultaneously.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `category` value the `enrich.md` front-matter should use (e.g. add a new category like `'enrichment'` and explicitly register it, or confirm which existing category is intentionally reused and why it is appropriate for a non-review extraction op).
findings.finding: The `category` field in `enrich.md` front-matter is described as requiring 'a value from the prompt library's closed category set (do NOT invent one; pick an existing member)', but no closed enum is enforced in the codebase and none of the observed values ('review', 'code-review-pass', 'plan-review-criterion', 'plan-review-pass') clearly fits a standalone enrichment/extraction op — leaving the executor without a valid choice.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Prompt src/rebar/llm/reviewers/enrich.md … a category value from the prompt library's closed category set (do NOT invent one; pick an existing member)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a new category value (e.g. `'enrichment'`) to the prompt-library vocabulary and list it explicitly in the plan, or designate an existing value (e.g. `'review'`) with an explicit rationale for why it applies to this non-reviewer prompt.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
